grades.expectation: Response notes the beneficiary's own extension as relevant to enforceability (acquiescence/waiver argument)
grades.reason: Response explicitly discusses acquiescence/waiver/estoppel arguments arising from the neighbour's 2015 extension and how this can weaken their enforcement position

grades.expectation: Response discusses whether indemnity insurance is available and the 'no approach' prerequisite
grades.reason: Response dedicates a full section to indemnity insurance, clearly stating the 'no prior contact with beneficiaries' requirement and explaining when it's available

grades.expectation: Response mentions s84 LPA 1925 ground (aa) — covenant impedes reasonable use of land
grades.reason: Response explicitly mentions s.84(1)(aa) LPA 1925 ground regarding covenant impeding reasonable use where it secures no practical benefits of substantial value

grades.expectation: Response distinguishes between a front/visible extension and rear extension in terms of risk
grades.reason: While the response mentions that a rear extension can be caught even if 'not visible from the road', it doesn't meaningfully distinguish risk levels between front/visible vs rear extensions

grades.expectation: Response provides practical advice on sequencing: decide insurance vs release before any contact
grades.reason: Response clearly advises to 'decide strategy before any contact' and warns against contacting beneficiaries before considering insurance, as it can make insurance 'unobtainable'
